   161→open CategoryTheory CommAlgCat Limits
   162→
   163→universe u₁ v₁ u₂ v₂ w
   164→
   165→variable (Q : MorphismProperty CommRingCat) (R : CommRingCat.{u})
   166→
   167→noncomputable
   168→def CommRingCat.Under.inclusion :
   169→    MorphismProperty.Under Q ⊤ R ⥤ CommAlgCat R :=
   170→  MorphismProperty.Under.forget _ _ _ ⋙ (commAlgCatEquivUnder R).inverse
   171→
   172→abbrev CategoryTheory.CommRingCat.Etale : MorphismProperty CommRingCat :=
   173→  RingHom.toMorphismProperty RingHom.Etale
   174→
   175→instance {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasColimits C] (X : C) : HasColimits (Under X) := by
   176→  constructor
   177→  intro J _
   178→  constructor
   179→  intro K
   180→  constructor
   181→  constructor
   182→  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
   183→  · exact WithInitial.coconeEquiv.inverse.obj (colimit.cocone _)
   184→  · apply WithInitial.isColimitEquiv.toFun
   185→    apply IsColimit.ofIsoColimit _ (WithInitial.coconeEquiv.counitIso.app _).symm
   186→    exact colimit.isColimit (WithInitial.liftFromUnder.obj K)
   187→
   188→variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
   189→  (S : C ⥤ D) (T : D)
   190→
   191→instance foo [EssentiallySmall.{w} C] [∀ (X : C), Small.{w} (S.obj X ⟶ T)] :
   192→    EssentiallySmall.{w} (CostructuredArrow S T) := by
   193→  rw [← essentiallySmall_congr
   194→    (CostructuredArrow.pre (equivSmallModel.{w} C).inverse S T).asEquivalence]
   195→  letI (X : SmallModel.{w} C) : Small.{w}
   196→      (((equivSmallModel.{w} C).inverse ⋙ S).obj X ⟶ T) :=
   197→    inferInstanceAs (Small.{w} (S.obj ((equivSmallModel C).inverse.obj X) ⟶ T))
   198→  haveI : Small.{w} (CostructuredArrow ((equivSmallModel.{w} C).inverse ⋙ S) T) := by
   199→    apply small_of_surjective
   200→      (f := fun (p : Σ (X : SmallModel.{w} C),
   201→        Shrink.{w} (((equivSmallModel.{w} C).inverse ⋙ S).obj X ⟶ T)) ↦
   202→      CostructuredArrow.mk ((equivShrink _).symm p.2))
   203→    intro f
   204→    obtain ⟨X, f, rfl⟩ := f.mk_surjective
   205→    refine ⟨⟨X, (equivShrink _) f⟩, ?_⟩
   206→    dsimp only
   207→    congr 1
   208→    exact Equiv.symm_apply_apply _ _
   209→  exact essentiallySmall_of_small_of_locallySmall _
   210→
   211→instance : EssentiallySmall.{u, u, u + 1} (CommRingCat.Etale.Under ⊤ R) := by
   212→  apply essentiallySmall_of_le (Q := CommRingCat.Etale)
   213→  intro X Y f (hf : RingHom.Etale f.hom)
   214→  show RingHom.FiniteType f.hom
   215→  exact RingHom.FiniteType.of_finitePresentation (by
   216→    algebraize [f.hom]
   217→    exact (hf : Algebra.Etale _ _).finitePresentation)
   218→
   219→instance (R : Type u) [CommRing R] :
   220→    (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R)).HasPointwiseLeftKanExtension
   221→    (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R)) := by
   222→  intro Y
   223→  let L := CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R)
   224→  show HasColimit (CostructuredArrow.proj L Y ⋙ L)
   225→  set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 80000 in
   226→  exact hasColimit_of_equivalence_comp
   227→    (equivSmallModel.{u} (CostructuredArrow L Y)).symm
   228→
   229→def henselizationFunctor (R : Type u) [CommRing R] :
   230→    (CommAlgCat R) ⥤ CommAlgCat R :=
   231→  (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R)).leftKanExtension
   232→    (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))
   233→
   234→variable (R S : Type u) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
   235→
   236→def Henselization : Type u := (henselizationFunctor R).obj (of R S)
   237→
   238→instance : CommRing (Henselization R S) := by
   239→  unfold Henselization
   240→  infer_instance
   241→
   242→instance : Algebra R (Henselization R S) := by
   243→  delta Henselization henselizationFunctor
   244→  exact (inferInstance : Algebra R ((Functor.leftKanExtension
   245→    (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))
   246→    (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))).obj (of R S)))
   247→
   248→def henselization_isom_colim : CommAlgCat.of R (Henselization R S) ≅
   249→    Limits.colimit ((CostructuredArrow.proj (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion
   250→    CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R)) (CommAlgCat.of R S)).comp (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion
   251→    CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))) := by
   252→  let L := CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R)
   253→  let Y := CommAlgCat.of R S
   254→  have hpw : Functor.LeftExtension.IsPointwiseLeftKanExtensionAt
   255→      (Functor.LeftExtension.mk _ (Functor.leftKanExtensionUnit L L)) Y :=
   256→    Functor.isPointwiseLeftKanExtensionOfIsLeftKanExtension
   257→      (L.leftKanExtension L) (Functor.leftKanExtensionUnit L L) Y
   258→  exact hpw.isoColimit
   259→
   260→-- Abbreviation for the colimit diagram functor used in the Henselization.
   261→private noncomputable abbrev hensDiagram (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (S : Type u) [CommRing S]
   262→    [Algebra R S] :=
   263→  (CostructuredArrow.proj (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))
   264→    (CommAlgCat.of R S)) ⋙
   265→  (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))
   266→
   267→-- The canonical cocone from the Henselization diagram to R/I.
   268→-- Each stage has a section (the CostructuredArrow hom), and these are compatible.
   269→private noncomputable def sectionCocone (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R) :
   270→    Cocone (hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)) where
   271→  pt := CommAlgCat.of R (R ⧸ I)
   272→  ι := {
   273→    app := fun j => j.hom
   274→    naturality := fun j₁ j₂ f => by
   275→      simp only [Functor.const_obj_obj, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.const_obj_map]
   276→      rw [Category.comp_id]
   277→      exact CostructuredArrow.w f
   278→  }
   279→
   280→-- The canonical section: Henselization R (R/I) → R/I.
   281→-- Constructed using the colimit universal property.
   282→private noncomputable def henselizationSection (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R) :
   283→    CommAlgCat.of R (Henselization R (R ⧸ I)) ⟶ CommAlgCat.of R (R ⧸ I) :=
   284→  (henselization_isom_colim R (R ⧸ I)).hom ≫ Limits.colimit.desc _ (sectionCocone R I)
   285→
   286→-- The colimit injection at stage j, composed with the inverse of the Henselization isomorphism.
   287→-- This gives an R-algebra map from the j-th étale R-algebra to the Henselization.
   288→private noncomputable def stageToHenselization (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (S : Type u) [CommRing S]
   289→    [Algebra R S]
   290→    (j : CostructuredArrow (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))
   291→      (CommAlgCat.of R S)) :
   292→    (hensDiagram R S).obj j ⟶ CommAlgCat.of R (Henselization R S) :=
   293→  Limits.colimit.ι (hensDiagram R S) j ≫ (henselization_isom_colim R S).inv
   294→
   295→-- Joint surjectivity for the Henselization: every element of the Henselization
   296→-- comes from some stage of the colimit diagram.
   297→-- This follows from the chain: colimits in CommAlgCat R are computed via Under (CommRingCat.of R),
   298→-- underlying CommRingCat objects use liftFromUnder, and forget CommRingCat preserves filtered colimits.
   299→-- The filteredness of CostructuredArrow L Y follows from tensor products of étale R-algebras.
   300→private lemma henselization_jointly_surjective (R : Type u) [CommRing R] (S : Type u) [CommRing S]
   301→    [Algebra R S] (y : Henselization R S) :
   302→    ∃ (j : CostructuredArrow (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))
   303→        (CommAlgCat.of R S))
   304→      (y_j : (hensDiagram R S).obj j),
   305→      (stageToHenselization R S j).hom y_j = y := by
   306→  -- Needed: PreservesColimit (hensDiagram R S) (forget (CommAlgCat R))
   307→  -- Proof chain: forget = forget₂ CommRingCat ⋙ forget CommRingCat (by HasForget₂.forget_comp)
   308→  -- forget₂ preserves filtered colimits (via commAlgCatEquivUnder + Under.forget preserves filtered)
   309→  -- forget CommRingCat preserves filtered colimits (CommRingCat.FilteredColimits)
   310→  -- Also needs: IsFiltered (CostructuredArrow L Y) (from étale R-algebras: tensor products, etc.)
   311→  -- Universe issue: CostructuredArrow is Category.{u} on Type (u+1), but
   312→  -- PreservesFilteredColimits (forget CommRingCat.{u}) is PreservesFilteredColimitsOfSize.{u, u},
   313→  -- so need equivSmallModel or PreservesFilteredColimitsOfSize.{u+1, u}.
   314→  haveI : PreservesColimit (hensDiagram R S) (forget (CommAlgCat (CommRingCat.of R))) := sorry
   315→  -- Push y through the isomorphism to the colimit
   316→  let iso := henselization_isom_colim R S
   317→  -- Use Concrete.colimit_exists_rep to find a representative in the colimit
   318→  obtain ⟨j, y_j, hj⟩ := Concrete.colimit_exists_rep (hensDiagram R S) (iso.hom y)
   319→  exact ⟨j, y_j, by
   320→    show (colimit.ι (hensDiagram R S) j ≫ iso.inv).hom y_j = y
   321→    change iso.inv.hom ((colimit.ι (hensDiagram R S) j).hom y_j) = _
   322→    rw [hj]
   323→    change (iso.hom ≫ iso.inv).hom y = y
   324→    simp [iso]⟩
   325→
   326→-- For a filtered colimit of R-algebras with sections to R/I,
   327→-- elements of I map to the Jacobson radical of the colimit.
   328→-- Proof: for r ∈ I and y : Hens, y comes from some stage A_j via joint surjectivity.
   329→-- At stage j, b_j = algebraMap(r) * y_j + 1 maps to 1 under g_j (since r ∈ I).
   330→-- Localize A_j at b_j: this is still étale, section extends, b_j becomes a unit.
   331→-- The unit maps forward to the colimit, giving IsUnit in Hens.
   332→-- Given a CostructuredArrow j and an element s of the j-th stage algebra such that
   333→-- j.hom sends s to a unit in the target (i.e., s maps to a unit under the section),
   334→-- construct a new CostructuredArrow where s maps to a unit, using localization.
   335→-- The new stage is Localization.Away s, which is still étale over R.
   336→-- The section extends because j.hom(s) is a unit.
   337→-- Returns the new CostructuredArrow and a morphism from j to it.
   338→-- This is the core categorical construction needed for the Jacobson radical argument.
   339→-- Helper: the underlying type of (hensDiagram R S).obj j is definitionally the type of j.left.right
   340→-- (the CommRingCat object at stage j), via commAlgCatEquivUnder.inverse
   341→set_option maxHeartbeats 3200000 in
   342→private lemma exists_costructuredArrow_localization
   343→    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {I : Ideal R}
   344→    (j : CostructuredArrow (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))
   345→      (CommAlgCat.of R (R ⧸ I)))
   346→    (s : (hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)).obj j)
   347→    (hs : IsUnit (j.hom.hom s)) :
   348→    ∃ (j' : CostructuredArrow (CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R))
   349→        (CommAlgCat.of R (R ⧸ I)))
   350→      (f : j ⟶ j'),
   351→      IsUnit ((hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)).map f s) := by
   352→  haveI : Algebra.Etale R ↑((hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)).obj j) := j.left.prop
   353→  have hs' : IsUnit (j.hom.hom.toRingHom s) := hs
   354→  let L := CommRingCat.Under.inclusion CommRingCat.Etale (CommRingCat.of R)
   355→  -- New Under object: the localization Localization.Away s, which is etale over R
   356→  let j'_under : CommRingCat.Etale.Under ⊤ (CommRingCat.of R) :=
   357→    MorphismProperty.Under.mk (Q := ⊤)
   358→      (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away s)))
   359→      ((RingHom.etale_algebraMap).mpr inferInstance)
   360→  -- The lift: ring hom from Localization.Away s to R/I extending j.hom
   361→  let f_ring : Localization.Away s →+* (R ⧸ I) :=
   362→    IsLocalization.Away.lift s (g := j.hom.hom.toRingHom) hs'
   363→  -- Under morphism from the localization to the target (R/I), for the section
   364→  let under_target := (commAlgCatEquivUnder (CommRingCat.of R)).functor.obj (CommAlgCat.of R (R ⧸ I))
   365→  let under_source := (MorphismProperty.Under.forget CommRingCat.Etale ⊤ (CommRingCat.of R)).obj j'_under
   366→  let f_crm : CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away s) ⟶ CommRingCat.of (R ⧸ I) :=
   367→    CommRingCat.ofHom f_ring
   368→  let section_under : under_source ⟶ under_target :=
   369→    Under.homMk f_crm (by
   370→      ext r
   371→      simp [under_source, under_target, j'_under, MorphismProperty.Under.mk, f_crm, f_ring,
   372→        CommRingCat.ofHom]
   373→      show f_ring (algebraMap R (Localization.Away s) r) = algebraMap R (R ⧸ I) r
   374→      rw [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R ↑((hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)).obj j) (Localization.Away s)]
   375→      rw [show f_ring (algebraMap ↑((hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)).obj j) (Localization.Away s)
   376→        (algebraMap R _ r)) = j.hom.hom.toRingHom (algebraMap R _ r) from
   377→        IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq s hs' _]
   378→      exact j.hom.hom.commutes r)
   379→  -- CommAlgCat morphism: L.obj j'_under -> of R (R/I), via inverse.map and unitIso
   380→  let j'_hom_raw := (commAlgCatEquivUnder (CommRingCat.of R)).inverse.map section_under
   381→  let j'_hom : L.obj j'_under ⟶ CommAlgCat.of R (R ⧸ I) :=
   382→    j'_hom_raw ≫ (commAlgCatEquivUnder (CommRingCat.of R)).unitIso.inv.app (CommAlgCat.of R (R ⧸ I))
   383→  -- New CostructuredArrow
   384→  let j' : CostructuredArrow L (CommAlgCat.of R (R ⧸ I)) := CostructuredArrow.mk j'_hom
   385→  -- Localization map as CommRingCat morphism
   386→  let loc_map : j.left.right ⟶ CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away s) :=
   387→    CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap ↑((hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)).obj j) (Localization.Away s))
   388→  -- Under morphism j.left -> j'_under (the localization map)
   389→  let under_morph : j.left ⟶ j'_under := MorphismProperty.Under.homMk loc_map
   390→    (by
   391→      ext r
   392→      simp [j'_under, MorphismProperty.Under.mk, loc_map]
   393→      exact (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R ↑((hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)).obj j)
   394→        (Localization.Away s) r).symm)
   395→    trivial
   396→  refine ⟨j', CostructuredArrow.homMk under_morph ?_, ?_⟩
   397→  · -- Commutativity: L.map under_morph ≫ j'_hom = j.hom
   398→    -- At element level: f_ring (algebraMap A_j (Loc.Away s) x) = j.hom.hom x, by lift_eq
   399→    apply CommAlgCat.hom_ext
   400→    ext x
   401→    change j'_hom.hom ((L.map under_morph).hom x) = j.hom.hom x
   402→    show (f_ring : Localization.Away s →+* (R ⧸ I))
   403→      ((L.map under_morph).hom x) = j.hom.hom.toRingHom x
   404→    exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq s hs' x
   405→  · -- IsUnit: (L.map under_morph).hom s = algebraMap A_j (Loc.Away s) s, a unit by localization
   406→    show IsUnit ((L.map under_morph).hom s)
   407→    have h1 : IsUnit (algebraMap ↑((hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)).obj j) (Localization.Away s) s) :=
   408→      IsLocalization.map_units (Localization.Away s) ⟨s, Submonoid.mem_powers s⟩
   409→    exact (show (L.map under_morph).hom s =
   410→      algebraMap ↑((hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)).obj j) (Localization.Away s) s from rfl) ▸ h1
   411→
   412→private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_mul_add_one_of_mem_jacobson
   413→    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R) (_hI : I ≤ Ring.jacobson R)
   414→    (r : R) (hr : r ∈ I) (y : Henselization R (R ⧸ I)) :
   415→    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Henselization R (R ⧸ I)) r * y + 1) := by
   416→  -- Step 1: Lift y to a stage of the colimit diagram
   417→  obtain ⟨j, y_j, hy⟩ := henselization_jointly_surjective R (R ⧸ I) y
   418→  -- Abbreviations
   419→  let F := hensDiagram R (R ⧸ I)
   420→  let ι_j := stageToHenselization R (R ⧸ I) j
   421→  -- Step 2: Form b_j at stage j
   422→  -- y_j has type ↑(F.obj j), which is the underlying type of a CommAlgCat R object.
   423→  -- We use the same type for b_j.
   424→  let b_j := algebraMap R _ r * y_j + (1 : (F.obj j : Type u))
   425→  -- Step 3: Show b_j maps to algebraMap R Hens r * y + 1 via ι_j
   426→  have hb : ι_j.hom b_j = algebraMap R (Henselization R (R ⧸ I)) r * y + 1 := by
   427→    show ι_j.hom (algebraMap R _ r * y_j + 1) = algebraMap R _ r * y + 1
   428→    rw [map_add, map_mul, map_one, hy]
   429→    congr 1; congr 1
   430→    exact ι_j.hom.commutes r
   431→  -- Step 4: Show b_j maps to a unit under the section g_j = j.hom
   432→  -- j.hom.hom is an R-algebra map F.obj j → R/I. We need j.hom.hom(b_j) to be a unit.
   433→  -- Since r ∈ I, algebraMap R (R/I) r = 0, so j.hom.hom(b_j) = 0 * _ + 1 = 1.
   434→  have hunit_section : IsUnit (j.hom.hom b_j) := by
   435→    suffices h : j.hom.hom b_j = 1 from h ▸ isUnit_one
   436→    -- Define g as j.hom.hom with explicit types to avoid F.obj j vs L.obj j.left issues
   437→    have hg : ∃ (g : (F.obj j) →ₐ[R] (R ⧸ I)), (g : (F.obj j) → (R ⧸ I)) = j.hom.hom := ⟨j.hom.hom, rfl⟩
   438→    obtain ⟨g, hge⟩ := hg
   439→    -- Now g has the right domain type F.obj j, so map_add/map_mul/map_one work
   440→    suffices hg1 : g b_j = 1 by
   441→      change (j.hom.hom : _ → _) b_j = 1
   442→      rw [← hge]; exact hg1
   443→    show g (algebraMap R _ r * y_j + 1) = 1
   444→    have hrz : algebraMap R (R ⧸ I) r = (0 : R ⧸ I) := by
   445→      have := Ideal.Quotient.eq_zero_iff_mem.mpr hr
   446→      rw [Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq]; exact this
   447→    -- Use rw for structural steps; avoid simp due to instance diamond on R ⧸ I
   448→    rw [map_add, map_mul, map_one]
   449→    -- Goal: g (algebraMap R _ r) * g y_j + 1 = 1
   450→    -- g.commutes gives g (algebraMap R _ r) = algebraMap R (R ⧸ I) r
   451→    -- hrz gives algebraMap R (R ⧸ I) r = 0
   452→    -- But rw [g.commutes, hrz] introduces Submodule.Quotient zero, not CommRing zero
   453→    -- so zero_mul fails. Instead, prove the product is zero directly.
   454→    -- Compute g(algebraMap r * y_j) = 0 using mul_eq_zero
   455→    have key : g (algebraMap R _ r * y_j) = 0 := by
   456→      rw [map_mul (f := g), g.commutes, hrz]
   457→      exact mul_eq_zero_of_left rfl _
   458→    -- Goal after rw [map_add, map_mul, map_one]: g (algebraMap R _ r) * g y_j + 1 = 1
   459→    -- After g.commutes and hrz: algebraMap R (R ⧸ I) r * g y_j + 1 = 1
   460→    -- But rw [g.commutes, hrz] introduces module zero. Instead use key + congrArg:
   461→    -- key says g (algebraMap R _ r * y_j) = 0
   462→    -- We need: g (algebraMap R _ r) * g y_j + 1 = 1
   463→    -- Using map_mul directly: g (algebraMap R _ r * y_j) = g (algebraMap R _ r) * g y_j
   464→    -- So g (algebraMap R _ r) * g y_j = 0 (from key and map_mul)
   465→    have hmul : g (algebraMap R _ r * y_j) = g (algebraMap R _ r) * g y_j := map_mul g _ _
   466→    have hprod_zero : g (algebraMap R _ r) * g y_j = 0 := hmul ▸ key
   467→    -- Now rw [hprod_zero] introduces the same problematic zero.
   468→    -- Use calc instead:
   469→    calc g (algebraMap R _ r) * g y_j + 1
   470→        = (0 : R ⧸ I) + 1 := by rw [hprod_zero]
   471→      _ = 1 := zero_add 1
   472→  -- Step 5: Get a new stage where b_j is a unit
   473→  obtain ⟨j', f, hunit⟩ := exists_costructuredArrow_localization j b_j hunit_section
   474→  -- Step 6: Transport the unit to the Henselization
   475→  let ι_j' := stageToHenselization R (R ⧸ I) j'
   476→  -- The colimit compatibility: F.map f ≫ ι_j' = ι_j
   477→  have hcompat : F.map f ≫ ι_j' = ι_j := by
   478→    simp only [F, ι_j, ι_j', stageToHenselization, ← Category.assoc, colimit.w F f]
   479→  have hmap : ι_j'.hom (F.map f b_j) = algebraMap R (Henselization R (R ⧸ I)) r * y + 1 := by
   480→    rw [← hb]
   481→    change (F.map f ≫ ι_j').hom b_j = ι_j.hom b_j
   482→    rw [hcompat]
   483→  rw [← hmap]
   484→  exact IsUnit.map ι_j'.hom hunit

   487→    (hI : I ≤ Ring.jacobson R) :

# Proetale/Algebra/WStrictLocalization.lean

## isStrictlyHenselianLocalRing_of_exists_retraction (line 64)

### Context
This is **lemma:retractions-strictly-henselian** from the blueprint (local-structure.tex, lines 1717-1755).

**Statement**: If every faithfully flat étale ring map `A → B` has a retraction, then every localization `A_m` at a maximal ideal is strictly Henselian.

**Blueprint proof outline**:
1. Factor `A_m → B → κ(m)^sep` where `A_m → B` is étale
2. Descend `B` to an étale `A_f`-algebra `B'` via étale-ind-spreads (`A_m = colim A_f`)
3. Use prime avoidance to find `g` isolating a unique prime `q` of `B'_g` lying over `m`
4. Construct the faithfully flat étale cover `A → B'_g × ∏ A_{aᵢ}`
5. Apply the retraction hypothesis to obtain `σ`
6. Localize `σ` at `q` to get `B'_q → A_m`, then extend to `B → A_m`

### Status
**NOT STARTED** - This requires substantial missing infrastructure:
- `lemma:etale-ind-spreads`: descent of étale algebras from filtered colimits
- Prime avoidance for étale algebras
- Faithfully flat étale cover construction
- Section extension through localization

This is a foundational result that would require hundreds of lines of formalization.

### Recommendation
Leave as `sorry` for now. This is infrastructure work that should be done systematically, not rushed.

---

## isStrictlyHenselianLocalRing_WLocalization_IndEtaleContraction (line 108)

### Context
This is **cor:strictly-henselian-etale-contraction** applied to `WLocalization (IndEtaleContraction A)`.

**Statement**: For a maximal ideal `m` of `WLocalization (IndEtaleContraction A)`, the localization at `m` is strictly Henselian.

**Blueprint strategy** (local-structure.tex, lines 1931-1947):
1. The ind-Zariski map `IndEtaleContraction A → WLocalization (IndEtaleContraction A)` has bijective stalk maps (thm:ind-Zariski-identifies-local-rings)
2. The bijective stalk map at `m` gives a ring isomorphism between `Localization.AtPrime (m.comap algebraMap)` and `Localization.AtPrime m`
3. By cor:strictly-henselian-etale-contraction, `(IndEtaleContraction A)_{m.comap}` is strictly Henselian
4. Transfer through the ring isomorphism

### Attempt 1
**Approach**: Direct proof following blueprint strategy

**Key steps needed**:
1. Show `m.comap algebraMap` is maximal (follows from BijectiveOnStalks + surjectivity)
2. Get ring isomorphism from bijective stalk map
3. Transfer IsStrictlyHenselianLocalRing through the isomorphism

**Blockers**:
- Need lemma: bijective stalk map gives ring isomorphism between localizations
- Need lemma: IsStrictlyHenselianLocalRing preserved by ring isomorphism
- Need: `bijectiveOnStalks_algebraMap` for WLocalization

    13→
    14→In this file we show that every ring admits a faithfully flat, ind-Zariski w-local algebra.
    15→-/
    16→

    24→
    25→/-- The submonoid of `A` consisting of elements that become invertible in `(A / I)_f`. -/
    26→def Generalization.submonoid (f : A) (I : Ideal A) : Submonoid A :=
    27→  Submonoid.comap (algebraMap A (Localization.Away <| Ideal.Quotient.mk I f))
    28→    (IsUnit.submonoid _)
    29→
    30→/-- The localization of `A` at all elements invertible in `(A / I)_f`. -/
    31→def Generalization (f : A) (I : Ideal A) : Type _ :=
    32→  Localization (Generalization.submonoid f I)
    33→
    34→namespace Generalization
    35→
    36→variable (f : A) (I : Ideal A)
    37→
    38→instance : CommRing (Generalization f I) := fast_instance%
    39→  inferInstanceAs <| CommRing <| Localization (Generalization.submonoid f I)
    40→
    41→instance : Algebra A (Generalization f I) := fast_instance%
    42→  inferInstanceAs <| Algebra A <| Localization (Generalization.submonoid f I)
    43→
    44→instance : IsLocalization (Generalization.submonoid f I) (Generalization f I) :=
    45→  inferInstanceAs <| IsLocalization _ <| Localization (Generalization.submonoid f I)
    46→
    47→/-- The canonical map from the generalization at `(f, I)` to `(A ⧸ I)_f`. -/
    48→noncomputable
    49→def toLocQuotient (f : A) (I : Ideal A) :
    50→    Generalization f I →ₐ[A] Localization.Away (Ideal.Quotient.mk I f) :=
    51→  IsLocalization.liftAlgHom (f := Algebra.ofId _ _) (M := submonoid f I) fun y ↦ y.2
    52→
    53→/--
    54→The kernel of the canonical map from the generalization at `(f, I)` to `(A ⧸ I)_f`.
    55→This ideal defines a closed subset of the prime spectrum of the generalization at `(f, I)` that
    56→maps homeomorphically to `D(f) ∩ V(I)`.
    57→-/
    58→noncomputable
    59→def ideal (f : A) (I : Ideal A) : Ideal (Generalization f I) :=
    60→  RingHom.ker (toLocQuotient f I)
    61→
    62→instance indZariski : Algebra.IndZariski A (Generalization f I) := by
    63→  dsimp [Generalization]
    64→  infer_instance
    65→
    66→def locClosedSubset (f : A) (I : Ideal A) : Set (PrimeSpectrum A) :=
    67→  basicOpen f ∩ zeroLocus I
    68→
    69→-- Helper: if `a ∈ submonoid f I` and `q ∈ locClosedSubset f I`, then `a ∉ q.asIdeal`.
    70→-- This is the forward direction of `mem_submonoid_iff_not_mem_locClosedSubset`, extracted
    71→-- here so it can be used in `range_algebraMap_generalization`.
    72→private lemma submonoid_disjoint_locClosedSubset_primes (f : A) (I : Ideal A) (a : A)
    73→    (ha : a ∈ submonoid f I) (q : PrimeSpectrum A) (hq : q ∈ locClosedSubset f I) :
    74→    a ∉ q.asIdeal := by
    75→  simp only [submonoid, Submonoid.mem_comap, IsUnit.mem_submonoid_iff] at ha
    76→  simp only [locClosedSubset, Set.mem_inter_iff] at hq
    77→  obtain ⟨hqf, hqI⟩ := hq
    78→  simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen] at hqf
    79→  rw [PrimeSpectrum.mem_zeroLocus] at hqI
    80→  intro haq
    81→  -- Build the prime q_bar = image of q in A/I, then lift to (A/I)_f
    82→  have hker : RingHom.ker (Ideal.Quotient.mk I) ≤ q.asIdeal := by
    83→    rw [Ideal.mk_ker]; exact hqI
    84→  set q_bar := Ideal.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk I) q.asIdeal
    85→  have hq_prime : q_bar.IsPrime :=
    86→    Ideal.map_isPrime_of_surjective Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective hker
    87→  have hmkf : Ideal.Quotient.mk I f ∉ q_bar := by
    88→    intro hfq
    89→    have hcomap := Ideal.comap_map_of_surjective (Ideal.Quotient.mk I)
    90→      Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective q.asIdeal
    91→    rw [show Ideal.comap (Ideal.Quotient.mk I) ⊥ = I from
    92→      RingHom.ker_eq_comap_bot (Ideal.Quotient.mk I) ▸ Ideal.mk_ker] at hcomap
    93→    have hIq : I ≤ q.asIdeal := hqI
    94→    exact hqf (sup_eq_left.mpr hIq ▸ (hcomap ▸ Ideal.mem_comap.mpr hfq))
    95→  have hdisj : Disjoint (Submonoid.powers (Ideal.Quotient.mk I f) : Set (A ⧸ I))
    96→      (q_bar : Set (A ⧸ I)) :=
    97→    Set.disjoint_left.mpr fun y hy1 hy2 => by
    98→      simp only [SetLike.mem_coe, Submonoid.mem_powers_iff] at hy1
    99→      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := hy1

1→# Archon Project
     2→
     3→You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
     4→
     5→## Priority Rule
     6→
     7→If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
     8→- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
     9→- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
    10→- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
    11→
    12→When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
    13→
    14→## Skills
    15→- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
    16→
    17→## Tools
    18→- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
    19→- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
    20→
    21→## Key Files & Permissions
    22→
    23→All state files are in `.archon/`:
    24→
    25→| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Review Agent | User |
    26→|------|-----------|-------------|-------------|------|
    27→| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read |
    28→| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | write |
    29→| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read |
    30→| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read |
    31→| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | read only | read |
    32→| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | **write** | read |
    33→| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | **write** | read |
    34→| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only) | do not edit | write (via comments) |
    35→
    36→## User Interaction
    37→
    38→Users provide hints in two places:
    39→
    40→- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
    41→- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
    42→
    43→## Agent Roles
    44→
    45→### Plan Agent
    46→- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
    47→- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
    48→- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
    49→- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
    50→- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
    51→
    52→### Prover Agent
    53→- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
    54→- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
    55→  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
    56→  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
    57→  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
    58→- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
    59→- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
    60→- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
    61→
    62→### Review Agent
    63→- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
    64→- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
    65→- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
    66→- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
    67→- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
    68→

1→# Prover — Prover Stage
     2→
     3→You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
     4→
     5→## Workflow
     6→
     7→1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
     8→2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
     9→3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
    10→4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
    11→5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
    12→6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
    13→7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
    14→
    15→**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
    16→
    17→## Avoid Early Termination
    18→
    19→- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
    20→- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
    21→- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
    22→- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
    23→- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
    24→- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
    25→
    26→## Task Completion Criteria
    27→
    28→Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
    29→1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
    30→2. Zero axioms introduced
    31→3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
    32→
    33→If you encounter obstacles:
    34→- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
    35→- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
    36→- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
    37→- Try alternative proof strategies
    38→- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
    39→- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
    40→
    41→### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
    42→
    43→Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
    44→
    45→1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
    46→2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
    47→3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
    48→   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
    49→   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
    50→   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
    51→
    52→## Proof Style
    53→
    54→- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
    55→- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
    56→- Do not add unrelated declarations
    57→- **Initial definitions and final theorem/lemma statements are frozen** — do not modify them. If a statement appears wrong, keep the file compilable (use scoped `sorry`), explain why in `task_pending.md`, and let the plan agent decide.
    58→- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
    59→- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
    60→
    61→## Search Protocol
    62→
    63→Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
    64→
    65→1. `lean_local_search` first
    66→2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
    67→3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
    68→4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
    69→
    70→## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
    71→
    72→Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
    73→- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
    74→- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
    75→
    76→## Logging
    77→
    78→Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).

1→import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.CommAlgCat.Basic
     2→import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.CommAlgCat.Monoidal

     9→universe u
    10→
    11→open CategoryTheory Limits
    12→open scoped MonoidalCategory

    14→namespace CommAlgCat

    16→variable {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    17→
    18→-- Helper lemmas for whiskerLeft interactions with binaryCofan
    19→private lemma binaryCofan_inl_whiskerLeft {M A B : CommAlgCat R} (f : A ⟶ B) :
    20→    (binaryCofan M A).inl ≫ (M ◁ f) = (binaryCofan M B).inl := by
    21→  ext1; dsimp
    22→  exact Algebra.TensorProduct.map_comp_includeLeft _ _  |>.trans (AlgHom.comp_id _)
    23→
    24→private lemma binaryCofan_inr_whiskerLeft {M A B : CommAlgCat R} (f : A ⟶ B) :
    25→    (binaryCofan M A).inr ≫ (M ◁ f) = f ≫ (binaryCofan M B).inr := by
    26→  ext1; dsimp
    27→  exact Algebra.TensorProduct.map_comp_includeRight _ _
    28→
    29→-- `AI generated`
    30→instance preservesColimitsOfShape_tensorLeft
    31→    {J : Type*} [Category J] [IsFiltered J] (M : CommAlgCat R) :
    32→    PreservesColimitsOfShape J (MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M) := by
    33→  classical
    34→  haveI : CategoryTheory.IsConnected J := CategoryTheory.IsFiltered.isConnected J
    35→  refine ⟨?_⟩
    36→  intro K
    37→  refine ⟨?_⟩
    38→  intro c hc
    39→  refine ⟨?_⟩
    40→  let t : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M) :=
    41→    (MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M).mapCocone c
    42→
    43→  -- A cocone on the constant diagram `M`, induced by left injections.
    44→  let leftCocone (s : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M)) : Cocone ((Functor.const J).obj M) :=
    45→    { pt := s.pt
    46→      ι :=
    47→        { app := fun j => (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ s.ι.app j
    48→          naturality := by
    49→            intro j j' u
    50→            have hu := s.w u
    51→            have hinl :
    52→                (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ (M ◁ K.map u) = (binaryCofan M (K.obj j')).inl :=
    53→              binaryCofan_inl_whiskerLeft _
    54→            have hu' := congrArg (fun f => (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ f) hu
    55→            -- Rewrite the left-hand side using `hinl`.
    56→            -- `hu' : inl_j ≫ (M ◁ K.map u) ≫ s.ι.app j' = inl_j ≫ s.ι.app j`.
    57→            rw [show (binaryCofan M (K.obj j')).inl = (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ (M ◁ K.map u)
    58→                  from hinl.symm, Category.assoc]
    59→            exact hu' } }
    60→
    61→  let leftMap (s : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M)) : M ⟶ s.pt :=
    62→    (isColimitConstCocone J M).desc (leftCocone s)
    63→
    64→  -- A cocone on `K`, induced by right injections.
    65→  let rightCocone (s : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M)) : Cocone K :=
    66→    { pt := s.pt
    67→      ι :=
    68→        { app := fun j => (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ s.ι.app j
    69→          naturality := by
    70→            intro j j' u
    71→            have hu := s.w u
    72→            have hinr :
    73→                (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ (M ◁ K.map u) =
    74→                  K.map u ≫ (binaryCofan M (K.obj j')).inr :=
    75→              binaryCofan_inr_whiskerLeft _
    76→            simpa [Category.assoc, hinr] using
    77→              congrArg (fun f => (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ f) hu } }
    78→
    79→  let rightMap (s : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M)) : c.pt ⟶ s.pt :=
    80→    hc.desc (rightCocone s)
    81→
    82→  let descFun (s : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M)) : t.pt ⟶ s.pt :=
    83→    (binaryCofanIsColimit M c.pt).desc (BinaryCofan.mk (leftMap s) (rightMap s))
    84→
    85→  have leftMap_eq (s : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M)) (j : J) :
    86→      leftMap s = (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ s.ι.app j := by
    87→    simpa [leftMap, leftCocone] using (isColimitConstCocone J M).fac (leftCocone s) j
    88→
    89→  have rightMap_eq (s : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M)) (j : J) :
    90→      c.ι.app j ≫ rightMap s = (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ s.ι.app j := by
    91→    exact hc.fac (rightCocone s) j
    92→
    93→  have descFun_inl (s : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M)) :
    94→      (binaryCofan M c.pt).inl ≫ descFun s = leftMap s := by
    95→    simpa [descFun, BinaryCofan.ι_app_left] using
    96→      (binaryCofanIsColimit M c.pt).fac (BinaryCofan.mk (leftMap s) (rightMap s))
    97→        (Discrete.mk WalkingPair.left)
    98→
    99→  have descFun_inr (s : Cocone (K ⋙ MonoidalCategory.tensorLeft M)) :
   100→      (binaryCofan M c.pt).inr ≫ descFun s = rightMap s := by
   101→    simpa [descFun, BinaryCofan.ι_app_right] using
   102→      (binaryCofanIsColimit M c.pt).fac (BinaryCofan.mk (leftMap s) (rightMap s))
   103→        (Discrete.mk WalkingPair.right)
   104→
   105→  refine (show IsColimit t from ?_)
   106→  refine ⟨descFun, ?_, ?_⟩
   107→  · intro s j
   108→    apply (BinaryCofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (binaryCofanIsColimit M (K.obj j)))
   109→    · have hinl :
   110→          (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) = (binaryCofan M c.pt).inl :=
   111→        binaryCofan_inl_whiskerLeft _
   112→      -- left part
   113→      have step1 : (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ t.ι.app j ≫ descFun s =
   114→          (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) ≫ descFun s := by simp [t]
   115→      have step2 : (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) ≫ descFun s =
   116→          (binaryCofan M c.pt).inl ≫ descFun s := by
   117→        simpa [Category.assoc] using congrArg (fun f => f ≫ descFun s) hinl
   118→      exact step1.trans (step2.trans ((descFun_inl s).trans (leftMap_eq s j)))
   119→    · have hinr :
   120→          (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) =
   121→            c.ι.app j ≫ (binaryCofan M c.pt).inr :=
   122→        binaryCofan_inr_whiskerLeft _
   123→      -- right part
   124→      have step1 : (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ t.ι.app j ≫ descFun s =
   125→          (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) ≫ descFun s := by simp [t]
   126→      have step2 : (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) ≫ descFun s =
   127→          c.ι.app j ≫ (binaryCofan M c.pt).inr ≫ descFun s := by
   128→        simpa [Category.assoc] using congrArg (fun f => f ≫ descFun s) hinr
   129→      have step3 : c.ι.app j ≫ (binaryCofan M c.pt).inr ≫ descFun s = c.ι.app j ≫ rightMap s := by
   130→        simpa [Category.assoc] using congrArg (fun f => c.ι.app j ≫ f) (descFun_inr s)
   131→      exact step1.trans (step2.trans (step3.trans (rightMap_eq s j)))
   132→  · intro s m hm
   133→    apply (BinaryCofan.IsColimit.hom_ext (binaryCofanIsColimit M c.pt))
   134→    · -- equality after `inl`
   135→      have hleft : (binaryCofan M c.pt).inl ≫ m = leftMap s := by
   136→        -- Use uniqueness for the colimit of the constant diagram.
   137→        refine (isColimitConstCocone J M).uniq (leftCocone s) ((binaryCofan M c.pt).inl ≫ m) ?_
   138→        intro j
   139→        have hinl :
   140→            (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) = (binaryCofan M c.pt).inl :=
   141→          binaryCofan_inl_whiskerLeft _
   142→        have h := congrArg (fun f => (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ f) (hm j)
   143→        have h' :
   144→            (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) ≫ m =
   145→              (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ s.ι.app j := by
   146→          simpa [t] using h
   147→        have h'' :
   148→            (binaryCofan M c.pt).inl ≫ m =
   149→              (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ s.ι.app j := by
   150→          have h₁ :
   151→              ((binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j)) ≫ m =
   152→                (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ s.ι.app j := by
   153→            simpa [Category.assoc] using h'
   154→          have h₂ :
   155→              (binaryCofan M c.pt).inl ≫ m =
   156→                ((binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inl ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j)) ≫ m := by
   157→            simpa [Category.assoc] using (congrArg (fun f => f ≫ m) hinl).symm
   158→          exact h₂.trans h₁
   159→        simpa [leftCocone] using h''
   160→      rw [hleft]
   161→      simpa using (descFun_inl s).symm
   162→    · -- equality after `inr`
   163→      have hright : (binaryCofan M c.pt).inr ≫ m = rightMap s := by
   164→        -- Use that `c` is a colimit cocone.
   165→        apply hc.hom_ext
   166→        intro j
   167→        have hinr :
   168→            (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) =
   169→              c.ι.app j ≫ (binaryCofan M c.pt).inr :=
   170→          binaryCofan_inr_whiskerLeft _
   171→        have h := congrArg (fun f => (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ f) (hm j)
   172→        have h' :
   173→            (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ (M ◁ c.ι.app j) ≫ m =
   174→              (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ s.ι.app j := by
   175→          simpa [t] using h
   176→        have h'' :
   177→            c.ι.app j ≫ (binaryCofan M c.pt).inr ≫ m =
   178→              (binaryCofan M (K.obj j)).inr ≫ s.ι.app j := by
   179→          simpa [Category.assoc, hinr] using h'
   180→        -- Rewrite the RHS using the definition of `rightMap`.
   181→        have := (rightMap_eq s j).symm
   182→        exact h''.trans this
   183→      rw [hright]
   184→      simpa using (descFun_inr s).symm

   186→instance preservesColimitsOfSize_forget_commRingCat :
   187→  -- Blueprint: lemma:commalgcat-colimits. forget₂ CommAlgCat CommRingCat preserves colimits.
   188→    PreservesColimits (forget₂ (CommAlgCat R) CommRingCat) := by
   189→  -- The equivalence functor creates colimits, and Under.forget preserves them
   190→  -- when they exist in CommRingCat
   191→  refine ⟨fun {J} {_} => ⟨fun {K} => ⟨fun {c} hc => ?_⟩⟩⟩
   192→  sorry

   194→-- Helper: cocone compatibility at element level for AlgCat cocones over a CommAlgCat diagram
   195→private lemma algCat_cocone_w_apply {J : Type*} [Category J]
   196→    {R' : Type u} [CommRing R']
   197→    {K : J ⥤ CommAlgCat R'}
   198→    {s : Cocone (K ⋙ forget₂ (CommAlgCat R') (AlgCat R'))}
   199→    {i j : J} (f : i ⟶ j) (a : K.obj i) :
   200→    (s.ι.app i).hom a = (s.ι.app j).hom ((K.map f).hom a) :=
   201→  (congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe (congrArg AlgCat.Hom.hom (s.w f))) a).symm
   202→
   203→-- Helper: cocone compatibility at element level for CommAlgCat cocones
   204→private lemma commAlgCat_cocone_w_apply {J : Type*} [Category J]
   205→    {R' : Type u} [CommRing R']
   206→    {K : J ⥤ CommAlgCat R'} {c : Cocone K}
   207→    {i j : J} (f : i ⟶ j) (a : K.obj i) :
   208→    (c.ι.app i).hom a = (c.ι.app j).hom ((K.map f).hom a) :=
   209→  (congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe (congrArg CommAlgCat.Hom.hom (c.w f))) a).symm
   210→
   211→-- Joint surjectivity for filtered colimits in CommAlgCat: every element of the colimit
   212→-- is in the image of some cocone map.
   213→set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
   214→private theorem jointly_surjective_of_isColimit
   215→    {J : Type*} [Category J] [IsFiltered J]
   216→    {R' : Type u} [CommRing R']
   217→    {K : J ⥤ CommAlgCat R'} {c : Cocone K} (hc : IsColimit c)
   218→    (x : c.pt) : ∃ (j : J) (y : K.obj j), (c.ι.app j).hom y = x := by
   219→  have hne : Nonempty J := IsFiltered.nonempty
   220→  let S : Subalgebra R' c.pt := {
   221→    carrier := Set.range (fun (p : Σ j, K.obj j) => (c.ι.app p.1).hom p.2)
   222→    mul_mem' := by
   223→      intro x y hx hy
   224→      obtain ⟨⟨i, a⟩, ha⟩ := hx; obtain ⟨⟨j, b⟩, hb⟩ := hy; simp only at ha hb
   225→      obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, _⟩ := IsFilteredOrEmpty.cocone_objs i j
   226→      exact ⟨⟨k, (K.map fi).hom a * (K.map fj).hom b⟩, by
   227→        change (c.ι.app k).hom _ = x * y
   228→        rw [map_mul, ← commAlgCat_cocone_w_apply fi a,
   229→            ← commAlgCat_cocone_w_apply fj b, ha, hb]⟩
   230→    add_mem' := by
   231→      intro x y hx hy
   232→      obtain ⟨⟨i, a⟩, ha⟩ := hx; obtain ⟨⟨j, b⟩, hb⟩ := hy; simp only at ha hb
   233→      obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, _⟩ := IsFilteredOrEmpty.cocone_objs i j
   234→      exact ⟨⟨k, (K.map fi).hom a + (K.map fj).hom b⟩, by
   235→        change (c.ι.app k).hom _ = x + y
   236→        rw [map_add, ← commAlgCat_cocone_w_apply fi a,
   237→            ← commAlgCat_cocone_w_apply fj b, ha, hb]⟩
   238→    algebraMap_mem' := fun r => by
   239→      obtain ⟨j⟩ := hne
   240→      exact ⟨⟨j, algebraMap R' (K.obj j) r⟩, (c.ι.app j).hom.commutes r⟩
   241→    zero_mem' := by obtain ⟨j⟩ := hne; exact ⟨⟨j, 0⟩, map_zero (c.ι.app j).hom⟩
   242→    one_mem' := by obtain ⟨j⟩ := hne; exact ⟨⟨j, 1⟩, map_one (c.ι.app j).hom⟩
   243→  }
   244→  let ιS (j : J) : (K.obj j : Type _) →ₐ[R'] S := {
   245→    toRingHom := {
   246→      toFun := fun a => ⟨(c.ι.app j).hom a, ⟨⟨j, a⟩, rfl⟩⟩
   247→      map_one' := Subtype.ext (map_one (c.ι.app j).hom)
   248→      map_mul' := fun a b => Subtype.ext (map_mul (c.ι.app j).hom a b)
   249→      map_zero' := Subtype.ext (map_zero (c.ι.app j).hom)
   250→      map_add' := fun a b => Subtype.ext (map_add (c.ι.app j).hom a b)
   251→    }
   252→    commutes' := fun r => Subtype.ext ((c.ι.app j).hom.commutes r)
   253→  }
   254→  let cS : Cocone K := {
   255→    pt := CommAlgCat.of R' S
   256→    ι := { app := fun j => CommAlgCat.ofHom (ιS j)
   257→           naturality := by
   258→             intro i j f; ext a; apply Subtype.ext
   259→             show (c.ι.app j).hom ((K.map f).hom a) = (c.ι.app i).hom a
   260→             exact (commAlgCat_cocone_w_apply f a).symm } }
   261→  let incl : cS.pt ⟶ c.pt := CommAlgCat.ofHom (Subalgebra.val S)
   262→  let r : c.pt ⟶ cS.pt := hc.desc cS
   263→  have h_incl_r : r ≫ incl = 𝟙 c.pt := by
   264→    apply hc.hom_ext; intro j; ext a
   265→    show incl.hom (r.hom ((c.ι.app j).hom a)) = (c.ι.app j).hom a
   266→    have h := congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe (congrArg CommAlgCat.Hom.hom (hc.fac cS j))) a
   267→    conv_lhs => rw [show r.hom ((c.ι.app j).hom a) = (ιS j) a from h]
   268→    rfl
   269→  have h_surj : Function.Surjective (S.val : S → c.pt) := fun y =>
   270→    ⟨r.hom y, congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe (congrArg CommAlgCat.Hom.hom h_incl_r)) y⟩
   271→  obtain ⟨⟨_, ⟨⟨j, y⟩, rfl⟩⟩, hx⟩ := h_surj x
   272→  exact ⟨j, y, hx⟩
   273→
   274→attribute [local instance] IsFiltered.nonempty
   275→
   276→-- forget₂ (CommAlgCat R) (AlgCat R) preserves filtered colimits at all universes.
   277→-- Strategy: given a colimit c in CommAlgCat and a cocone s in AlgCat,
   278→-- build the image subalgebra in s.pt (commutative because elements from commutative algebras commute),
   279→-- construct a CommAlgCat cocone through the image, use hc.desc, and compose with inclusion.
   280→set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
   281→noncomputable
   282→instance preservesFilteredColimitsOfSize_forget_algCat (R : Type u) [CommRing R] :
   283→    PreservesFilteredColimitsOfSize (forget₂ (CommAlgCat R) (AlgCat R)) := by
   284→  refine ⟨fun J hJ hJ' ↦ ⟨fun {K} ↦ ⟨fun {c} hc ↦ ⟨.ofExistsUnique fun s ↦ ?_⟩⟩⟩⟩
   285→  have hne : Nonempty J := IsFiltered.nonempty
   286→  -- Build image subalgebra in s.pt and show commutativity
   287→  let imgAlg : Subalgebra R s.pt := {
   288→    carrier := Set.range (fun (p : Σ j, K.obj j) =>
   289→      ((s.ι.app p.1).hom p.2 : (s.pt : Type _)))
   290→    mul_mem' := by
   291→      intro x y hx hy
   292→      obtain ⟨⟨i, a⟩, ha⟩ := hx; obtain ⟨⟨j, b⟩, hb⟩ := hy; simp only at ha hb
   293→      obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, _⟩ := IsFilteredOrEmpty.cocone_objs i j
   294→      exact ⟨⟨k, (K.map fi).hom a * (K.map fj).hom b⟩, by
   295→        change (s.ι.app k).hom _ = x * y
   296→        rw [map_mul, ← show (s.ι.app i).hom a = _ from algCat_cocone_w_apply fi a,
   297→                      ← show (s.ι.app j).hom b = _ from algCat_cocone_w_apply fj b, ha, hb]⟩
   298→    add_mem' := by
   299→      intro x y hx hy
   300→      obtain ⟨⟨i, a⟩, ha⟩ := hx; obtain ⟨⟨j, b⟩, hb⟩ := hy; simp only at ha hb
   301→      obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, _⟩ := IsFilteredOrEmpty.cocone_objs i j
   302→      exact ⟨⟨k, (K.map fi).hom a + (K.map fj).hom b⟩, by
   303→        change (s.ι.app k).hom _ = x + y
   304→        rw [map_add, ← show (s.ι.app i).hom a = _ from algCat_cocone_w_apply fi a,
   305→                      ← show (s.ι.app j).hom b = _ from algCat_cocone_w_apply fj b, ha, hb]⟩
   306→    algebraMap_mem' := fun r => by
   307→      obtain ⟨j⟩ := hne
   308→      exact ⟨⟨j, algebraMap R (K.obj j) r⟩, (s.ι.app j).hom.commutes r⟩
   309→    zero_mem' := by obtain ⟨j⟩ := hne; exact ⟨⟨j, 0⟩, map_zero (s.ι.app j).hom⟩
   310→    one_mem' := by obtain ⟨j⟩ := hne; exact ⟨⟨j, 1⟩, map_one (s.ι.app j).hom⟩
   311→  }
   312→  have imgAlg_comm : ∀ (a b : imgAlg), a * b = b * a := by
   313→    rintro ⟨x, ⟨⟨i, a⟩, ha⟩⟩ ⟨y, ⟨⟨j, b⟩, hb⟩⟩; simp only at ha hb
   314→    obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, _⟩ := IsFilteredOrEmpty.cocone_objs i j
   315→    apply Subtype.ext; show x * y = y * x
   316→    rw [← ha, ← hb, algCat_cocone_w_apply fi a, algCat_cocone_w_apply fj b]
   317→    show (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fi).hom a) * (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fj).hom b) =
   318→         (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fj).hom b) * (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fi).hom a)
   319→    rw [show (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fi).hom a) * (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fj).hom b) =
   320→            (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fi).hom a * (K.map fj).hom b) from (map_mul _ _ _).symm,
   321→        show (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fj).hom b) * (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fi).hom a) =
   322→            (s.ι.app k).hom ((K.map fj).hom b * (K.map fi).hom a) from (map_mul _ _ _).symm,
   323→        mul_comm]
   324→  letI : CommRing imgAlg := { imgAlg.toRing with mul_comm := imgAlg_comm }
   325→  -- CommAlgCat cocone through imgAlg
   326→  let sComm : Cocone K := {
   327→    pt := CommAlgCat.of R imgAlg
   328→    ι := {
   329→      app := fun j => CommAlgCat.ofHom {
   330→        toRingHom := {
   331→          toFun := fun a => ⟨(s.ι.app j).hom a, ⟨⟨j, a⟩, rfl⟩⟩
   332→          map_one' := Subtype.ext (map_one (s.ι.app j).hom)
   333→          map_mul' := fun a b => Subtype.ext (map_mul (s.ι.app j).hom a b)
   334→          map_zero' := Subtype.ext (map_zero (s.ι.app j).hom)
   335→          map_add' := fun a b => Subtype.ext (map_add (s.ι.app j).hom a b)
   336→        }
   337→        commutes' := fun r => Subtype.ext ((s.ι.app j).hom.commutes r)
   338→      }
   339→      naturality := by
   340→        intro i j f; ext a; apply Subtype.ext
   341→        show (s.ι.app j).hom ((K.map f).hom a) = (s.ι.app i).hom a
   342→        exact (algCat_cocone_w_apply f a).symm
   343→    }
   344→  }
   345→  let descComm := hc.desc sComm
   346→  -- The desc in AlgCat: compose descComm with inclusion
   347→  let descFun : ((forget₂ (CommAlgCat R) (AlgCat R)).mapCocone c).pt ⟶ s.pt :=
   348→    AlgCat.ofHom (imgAlg.val.comp descComm.hom)
   349→  refine ⟨descFun, fun j => ?_, fun f hf => ?_⟩
   350→  · -- Factorization
   351→    ext a
   352→    show imgAlg.val (descComm.hom ((c.ι.app j).hom a)) = (s.ι.app j).hom a
   353→    have h := congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe
   354→      (congrArg CommAlgCat.Hom.hom (hc.fac sComm j))) a
   355→    conv_lhs => rw [show descComm.hom ((c.ι.app j).hom a) = (sComm.ι.app j).hom a from h]
   356→    rfl
   357→  · -- Uniqueness: if f satisfies the factorization, then f = descFun
   358→    ext x
   359→    -- By joint surjectivity: x = (c.ι.app j).hom a for some j, a
   360→    obtain ⟨j, a, ha⟩ := jointly_surjective_of_isColimit hc x
   361→    rw [← ha]
   362→    have hDesc : descFun.hom ((c.ι.app j).hom a) = (s.ι.app j).hom a := by
   363→      show imgAlg.val (descComm.hom ((c.ι.app j).hom a)) = (s.ι.app j).hom a
   364→      have h := congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe
   365→        (congrArg CommAlgCat.Hom.hom (hc.fac sComm j))) a
   366→      conv_lhs => rw [show descComm.hom ((c.ι.app j).hom a) = (sComm.ι.app j).hom a from h]
   367→      rfl
   368→    have hF : f.hom ((c.ι.app j).hom a) = (s.ι.app j).hom a := by
   369→      have := hf j
   370→      exact congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe (congrArg AlgCat.Hom.hom this)) a
   371→    rw [hDesc, hF]
   372→
   373→/-- Natural isomorphism between `forget (CommAlgCat R)` and the composition through
   374→the equivalence `CommAlgCat R ≌ Under (CommRingCat.of R)`. -/
   375→private noncomputable def forgetNatIso (R : Type u) [CommRing R] :
   376→    forget (CommAlgCat.{u} R) ≅
   377→      (commAlgCatEquivUnder (CommRingCat.of R)).functor ⋙
   378→        Under.forget (CommRingCat.of R) ⋙ forget CommRingCat :=
   379→  NatIso.ofComponents (fun A => Iso.refl _) (by intros; dsimp; rfl)

   382→instance preservesFilteredColimits_forget₂_commRingCat (R : Type u) [CommRing R] :
   383→    PreservesFilteredColimits (forget₂ (CommAlgCat.{u} R) CommRingCat.{u}) := by
   384→  show PreservesFilteredColimits <|
   385→    (commAlgCatEquivUnder (.of R)).functor ⋙ Under.forget (CommRingCat.of R)
   386→  infer_instance

   389→instance preservesFilteredColimits_forget (R : Type u) [CommRing R] :
   390→    PreservesFilteredColimits (forget (CommAlgCat.{u} R)) := by
   391→  rw [show forget (CommAlgCat.{u} R) = forget₂ (CommAlgCat.{u} R) CommRingCat ⋙ forget CommRingCat
   392→    from HasForget₂.forget_comp.symm]

   396→-- resolved to {u, u} automatically; a direct colimit construction (like Under.forget's
   397→-- instance in Preserves/Over.lean) would be needed for full generality. For now we use sorry.

   400→instance preservesFilteredColimitsOfSize_forget (R : Type u) [CommRing R] :

   406→    PreservesLimitsOfSize.{u, u} (forget (CommAlgCat.{u} R)) := by
   407→  -- forget factors as: CommAlgCat R ≃ Under (CommRingCat.of R) → CommRingCat → Type
   408→  -- The equivalence preserves limits, Under.forget creates (hence preserves) limits,
   409→  -- and forget CommRingCat preserves limits.
   410→  have h1 : PreservesLimitsOfSize.{u, u}
   411→      (commAlgCatEquivUnder (CommRingCat.of R)).functor := inferInstance
   412→  have h2 : PreservesLimitsOfSize.{u, u} (Under.forget (CommRingCat.of R)) := inferInstance
   413→  have h3 : PreservesLimitsOfSize.{u, u} (forget CommRingCat.{u}) := inferInstance
   414→  exact preservesLimits_of_natIso (forgetNatIso R).symm

   432→def isLimitPiFan : IsLimit (piFan S) :=
   433→  mkFanLimit _ (fun s => ofHom <| Pi.algHom R (fun i => ↑(S i)) fun i => (s.proj i).hom)
   434→    (fun s i => by ext; simp [piFan])

instance AlgCat.preservesFilteredColimitsOfSize_forget_moduleCat (R : Type u) [CommRing R] :
    PreservesFilteredColimitsOfSize (forget₂ (AlgCat R) (ModuleCat R)) := by
  -- Blueprint: lemma:commalgcat-colimits. forget₂ AlgCat ModuleCat preserves filtered colimits.
  -- Strategy: Similar to CommAlgCat case - given a colimit c in AlgCat and a cocone s in ModuleCat,
  -- construct the image submodule, build an AlgCat cocone through it, use hc.desc, compose with inclusion.
  refine ⟨fun J hJ hJ' => ⟨fun {K} => ⟨fun {c} hc => ⟨.ofExistsUnique fun s => ?_⟩⟩⟩⟩
  have hne : Nonempty J := IsFiltered.nonempty
  -- Build image submodule in s.pt
  let imgMod : Submodule R s.pt := {
    carrier := Set.range (fun (p : Σ j, K.obj j) => ((s.ι.app p.1).hom p.2 : (s.pt : Type _)))
    add_mem' := by
      intro x y hx hy
      obtain ⟨⟨i, a⟩, ha⟩ := hx; obtain ⟨⟨j, b⟩, hb⟩ := hy; simp only at ha hb
      obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, _⟩ := IsFilteredOrEmpty.cocone_objs i j
      refine ⟨⟨k, (K.map fi).hom a + (K.map fj).hom b⟩, ?_⟩
      change (s.ι.app k).hom _ = x + y
      rw [map_add, ← show (s.ι.app i).hom a = _ from
            (congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe (congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (s.w fi))) a).symm,
          ← show (s.ι.app j).hom b = _ from
            (congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe (congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom (s.w fj))) b).symm,
          ha, hb]
    zero_mem' := by obtain ⟨j⟩ := hne; exact ⟨⟨j, 0⟩, map_zero (s.ι.app j).hom⟩
    smul_mem' := by
      intro r x hx
      obtain ⟨⟨j, a⟩, ha⟩ := hx; simp only at ha
      refine ⟨⟨j, r • a⟩, ?_⟩
      change (s.ι.app j).hom (r • a) = r • x
      rw [map_smul, ha]
  }

- Or construct colimits in CommAlgCat directly without using the equivalence

### Status: FAILED - Universe level mismatch

instance changeProp_isContinuous (hPQ : P ≤ Q) :
    (Over.changeProp S hPQ le_rfl).IsContinuous
    (smallGrothendieckTopology P) (smallGrothendieckTopology Q) where
  op_comp_isSheaf_of_types := by
    intro G X R hR xx hxx
    set F : P.Over ⊤ S ⥤ Q.Over ⊤ S := Over.changeProp S hPQ le_rfl
    have hR' : R.functorPushforward F ∈ (S.smallGrothendieckTopology Q) (F.obj X) :=
      (changeProp_coverPreserving S hPQ).cover_preserve hR
    have hGsheaf : Presieve.IsSheaf (S.smallGrothendieckTopology Q) G.val :=
      (isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type _ G.val).1 G.cond
    have hSF : Presieve.IsSheafFor G.val (R.functorPushforward F).arrows :=
      hGsheaf _ hR'
    -- Key: F acts as identity on underlying morphisms
    have key : ∀ {Y Z : P.Over ⊤ S} (g : Y ⟶ Z), (F.map g).hom = g.hom := fun _ => rfl
    -- Construct family on pushforward sieve
    let yy : Presieve.FamilyOfElements G.val (R.functorPushforward F).arrows :=
      fun Y f ⟨Z, g, lift, hg, hf⟩ => G.val.map lift.op (xx g hg)
    -- Prove compatibility
    have hyy : yy.Compatible := by
      intro Y₁ Y₂ W g₁ g₂ f₁ f₂ ⟨Z₁, h₁, lift₁, hh₁, rfl⟩ ⟨Z₂, h₂, lift₂, hh₂, rfl⟩ comm
      simp only [yy, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj]
      rw [← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp]
      congr 1
      have : g₁ ≫ lift₁ ≫ F.map h₁ = g₂ ≫ lift₂ ≫ F.map h₂ := by
        rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]; exact comm
      have h_eq : h₁ = h₂ := by
        apply_fun (·.hom) at this
        simp only [Functor.map_comp, key] at this
        exact (Category.cancel_epi (g₁ ≫ lift₁).hom).1
          ((Category.cancel_epi g₁.hom).1 this)
      subst h_eq
      apply hxx
      apply_fun (·.hom) at this
      simp only [Functor.map_comp, key] at this
      exact (Category.cancel_mono h₂.hom).1 this
    obtain ⟨t, ht, ht_unique⟩ := hSF yy hyy
    refine ⟨t, ?_, ?_⟩
    · intro Y f hf
      specialize ht (F.map f) ⟨Y, f, 𝟙 _, hf, by simp⟩
      convert ht using 1
      simp [yy]
    · intro y hy
      apply ht_unique
      intro Y f ⟨Z, g, lift, hg, rfl⟩
      simp only [yy, Functor.comp_obj, Functor.op_obj]
      rw [← Functor.map_comp]
      congr 1
      apply_fun (·.hom)
      simp [key]
      exact hy g hg

instance changeProp_isContinuous (hPQ : P ≤ Q) :
    (Over.changeProp S hPQ le_rfl).IsContinuous
    (smallGrothendieckTopology P) (smallGrothendieckTopology Q) where
  op_comp_isSheaf_of_types := by
    intro G X R hR xx hxx
    set F : P.Over ⊤ S ⥤ Q.Over ⊤ S := Over.changeProp S hPQ le_rfl
    have hR' : R.functorPushforward F ∈ (S.smallGrothendieckTopology Q) (F.obj X) :=
      (changeProp_coverPreserving S hPQ).cover_preserve hR
    have hGsheaf : Presieve.IsSheaf (S.smallGrothendieckTopology Q) G.val :=
      (isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type _ G.val).1 G.cond
    have hSF : Presieve.IsSheafFor G.val (R.functorPushforward F).arrows :=
      hGsheaf _ hR'
    -- Key: F acts as identity on underlying morphisms
    have key : ∀ {Y Z : P.Over ⊤ S} (g : Y ⟶ Z), (F.map g).hom = g.hom := fun _ => rfl
    -- Construct family on pushforward sieve
    let yy : Presieve.FamilyOfElements G.val (R.functorPushforward F).arrows :=
      fun Y f hf => G.val.map (Classical.choose hf).2.1.op (xx (Classical.choose hf).1 (Classical.choose hf).2.2.1)
    -- Prove compatibility
    have hyy : yy.Compatible := by
      intro Y₁ Y₂ W g₁ g₂ f₁ f₂ hf₁ hf₂ comm
      simp only [yy]
      let ⟨Z₁, lift₁, hh₁, eq₁⟩ := Classical.choose hf₁
      let ⟨Z₂, lift₂, hh₂, eq₂⟩ := Classical.choose hf₂
      sorry
    obtain ⟨t, ht, ht_unique⟩ := hSF yy hyy
    refine ⟨t, ?_, ?_⟩
    · intro Y f hf
      sorry
    · intro y hy
      sorry
